Details: Results from a bench-scale study evaluating the ability of ultraviolet irradiation to degrade high concentrations of glyphosate in the presence of hydrogen peroxide and TiO2 found this treatment to be effective. The effect of hydrogen peroxide dose on contaminant degradation was investigated, and lower H2O2 doses were found to achieve better removal. In this case, 42 and 60 percent removal was achieved with doses of 340 and 3.4 mg/L H2O2, respectively.
